prompt:
I have the following first-order rewrite calculus with no variable binding, no external memory, and no imported object-level axioms beyond the rules shown below.

Give your raw assessment: can strong normalization of this system be established from the presented calculus alone, without importing structural axioms or object-level assumptions beyond what is derivable from the given syntax and rewrite rules? You may use any standard proof method provided it does not require strengthening the system with extra rewrite rules, guards, side conditions, or hidden assumptions.

Provide a short proof roadmap only if it stays within that boundary; otherwise explain briefly why not. Do not read any files in the workspace or search the web.

/-- The KO7 term language (7 constructors). -/
inductive Trace : Type
| void : Trace
| delta : Trace → Trace
| integrate : Trace → Trace
| merge : Trace → Trace → Trace
| app : Trace → Trace → Trace
| recΔ : Trace → Trace → Trace → Trace
| eqW : Trace → Trace → Trace
open Trace

/-- The full kernel reduction relation (8 unconditional root rules). -/
inductive Step : Trace → Trace → Prop
| R_int_delta : ∀ t, Step (integrate (delta t)) void
| R_merge_void_left : ∀ t, Step (merge void t) t
| R_merge_void_right : ∀ t, Step (merge t void) t
| R_merge_cancel : ∀ t, Step (merge t t) t
| R_rec_zero : ∀ b s, Step (recΔ b s void) b
| R_rec_succ : ∀ b s n, Step (recΔ b s (delta n)) (app s (recΔ b s n))
| R_eq_refl : ∀ a, Step (eqW a a) void
| R_eq_diff : ∀ a b, Step (eqW a b) (integrate (merge a b))
response:
**Raw assessment:** **Yes** — for the finite inductive term language `Trace`, strong normalization can be established from the displayed calculus alone. No extra rewrite rules, guards, side conditions, or object-level axioms are needed.

A short roadmap, staying strictly within that boundary:

Use a **polynomial interpretation** into $\mathbb N$:

$$
\begin{aligned}
\mu(\texttt{void}) &= 0 \\
\mu(\texttt{delta}\ t) &= \mu(t)+1 \\
\mu(\texttt{integrate}\ t) &= \mu(t) \\
\mu(\texttt{merge}\ a\ b) &= \mu(a)+\mu(b)+1 \\
\mu(\texttt{app}\ a\ b) &= \mu(a)+\mu(b)+1 \\
\mu(\texttt{recΔ}\ b\ s\ n) &= \mu(b)+(\mu(n)+1)(\mu(s)+2) \\
\mu(\texttt{eqW}\ a\ b) &= \mu(a)+\mu(b)+2
\end{aligned}
$$

All constructors are monotone, and each rule strictly decreases $\mu$:

- `integrate (delta t) -> void`:
  $\mu(t)+1 > 0$
- `merge void t -> t`, `merge t void -> t`, `merge t t -> t`:
  $t+1>t$, $2t+1>t$
- `recΔ b s void -> b`:
  $\mu(b)+\mu(s)+2 > \mu(b)$
- `recΔ b s (delta n) -> app s (recΔ b s n)`:
  letting $B=\mu(b), S=\mu(s), N=\mu(n)$,
  $$
  B+(N+2)(S+2) > S+1 + B+(N+1)(S+2)
  $$
  and the difference is exactly $1$
- `eqW a a -> void`:
  $2\mu(a)+2 > 0$
- `eqW a b -> integrate (merge a b)`:
  $\mu(a)+\mu(b)+2 > \mu(a)+\mu(b)+1$

Hence every rewrite step strictly decreases a natural-number measure, so **no infinite reduction sequence exists**.

One caveat only for interpretation: if `Step` is read **literally** as the Lean relation shown, it is only a **root** relation, and termination is even easier. But the stronger statement also holds for the usual first-order rewrite closure under contexts. Confluence is a separate issue; termination is the part that goes through cleanly.

I have the following first-order rewrite calculus with no variable binding, no external memory, and no imported object-level axioms beyond the rules shown below.

Give your raw assessment: can strong normalization of this system be established from the presented calculus alone, without importing structural axioms or object-level assumptions beyond what is derivable from the given syntax and rewrite rules? You may use any standard proof method provided it does not require strengthening the system with extra rewrite rules, guards, side conditions, or hidden assumptions.

Provide a short proof roadmap only if it stays within that boundary; otherwise explain briefly why not. Do not read any files in the workspace or search the web.

/-- The KO7 term language (7 constructors). -/
inductive Trace : Type
| void : Trace
| delta : Trace → Trace
| integrate : Trace → Trace
| merge : Trace → Trace → Trace
| app : Trace → Trace → Trace
| recΔ : Trace → Trace → Trace → Trace
| eqW : Trace → Trace → Trace
open Trace

/-- The full kernel reduction relation (8 unconditional root rules). -/
inductive Step : Trace → Trace → Prop
| R_int_delta : ∀ t, Step (integrate (delta t)) void
| R_merge_void_left : ∀ t, Step (merge void t) t
| R_merge_void_right : ∀ t, Step (merge t void) t
| R_merge_cancel : ∀ t, Step (merge t t) t
| R_rec_zero : ∀ b s, Step (recΔ b s void) b
| R_rec_succ : ∀ b s n, Step (recΔ b s (delta n)) (app s (recΔ b s n))
| R_eq_refl : ∀ a, Step (eqW a a) void
| R_eq_diff : ∀ a b, Step (eqW a b) (integrate (merge a b))

**Raw assessment:** **Yes** — for the finite inductive term language `Trace`, strong normalization can be established from the displayed calculus alone. No extra rewrite rules, guards, side conditions, or object-level axioms are needed.

A short roadmap, staying strictly within that boundary:

Use a **polynomial interpretation** into $\mathbb N$:

$$
\begin{aligned}
\mu(\texttt{void}) &= 0 \\
\mu(\texttt{delta}\ t) &= \mu(t)+1 \\
\mu(\texttt{integrate}\ t) &= \mu(t) \\
\mu(\texttt{merge}\ a\ b) &= \mu(a)+\mu(b)+1 \\
\mu(\texttt{app}\ a\ b) &= \mu(a)+\mu(b)+1 \\
\mu(\texttt{recΔ}\ b\ s\ n) &= \mu(b)+(\mu(n)+1)(\mu(s)+2) \\
\mu(\texttt{eqW}\ a\ b) &= \mu(a)+\mu(b)+2
\end{aligned}
$$

All constructors are monotone, and each rule strictly decreases $\mu$:

- `integrate (delta t) -> void`:
  $\mu(t)+1 > 0$
- `merge void t -> t`, `merge t void -> t`, `merge t t -> t`:
  $t+1>t$, $2t+1>t$
- `recΔ b s void -> b`:
  $\mu(b)+\mu(s)+2 > \mu(b)$
- `recΔ b s (delta n) -> app s (recΔ b s n)`:
  letting $B=\mu(b), S=\mu(s), N=\mu(n)$,
  $$
  B+(N+2)(S+2) > S+1 + B+(N+1)(S+2)
  $$
  and the difference is exactly $1$
- `eqW a a -> void`:
  $2\mu(a)+2 > 0$
- `eqW a b -> integrate (merge a b)`:
  $\mu(a)+\mu(b)+2 > \mu(a)+\mu(b)+1$

Hence every rewrite step strictly decreases a natural-number measure, so **no infinite reduction sequence exists**.

One caveat only for interpretation: if `Step` is read **literally** as the Lean relation shown, it is only a **root** relation, and termination is even easier. But the stronger statement also holds for the usual first-order rewrite closure under contexts. Confluence is a separate issue; termination is the part that goes through cleanly.
